_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Resolve allegations of non-compliance with the corporate privacy policies or notice of information practices.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to identify and resolve conflicts between data monetization objectives and data protection, privacy, and ethical obligations.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-A-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-007 (RC, cosine: 0.479), RC-T-003 (RC, cosine: 0.471).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.0/10 (raw 0.602). Shared vocabulary: [privacy, resolve]. DCWF-only terms [allegations, compliance, corporate, information, non, notice] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, conflicts, data, ethical, identify, monetization] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.871 (P=0.872, R=0.870). Balanced P/R — the 14-word DCWF element and 17-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.4%). "Resolve allegations of non-compliance with the corporate privacy policies or not" and "Ability to identify and resolve conflicts between data monetization objectives a" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.499 < STS 0.602 (gap -0.103). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [allegations, compliance, corporate] and [ability, conflicts, data] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.7%. Shared tokens: [privacy, resolve]. DCWF-only: [allegations, compliance, corporate, information, non, notice]. WIDAI-only: [ability, conflicts, data, ethical, identify, monetization].
